name: mobai-mcp
readme_summary: The MobAI MCP Server enables AI assistants to control Android and iOS devices, emulators, and simulators through a unified DSL-first interface. It simplifies device interaction by batching commands via a single `execute_dsl` tool, which handles complex sequences of actions, predicates, assertions, and waits server-side.
key_features: Control Android and iOS devices, emulators, and simulators
Batch execution of device interactions via `execute_dsl`
Device management tools (list, get, start/stop bridge)
Screenshot capabilities (low-quality for LLM, high-quality for reporting)
primary_use_case: To provide a standardized and efficient way for AI assistants to automate and test mobile applications on various devices and platforms.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled puppeteer standing behind a large, intricate control panel filled with glowing buttons and levers. The puppeteer's hands are manipulating the controls, which are directly connected by luminous threads to miniature, realistic Android and iOS devices floating in front of the panel. Each device is performing a different action, like tapping an icon or swiping a screen, as dictated by the controls. Subtle digital particles emanate from the control panel, representing the DSL commands. The scene is set in a clean, modern workshop with soft, focused lighting. The MobAI MCP Server logo is subtly integrated into the control panel design. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing the connection between the AI's commands and the device actions.

name: glance
description: A self-hosted dashboard that puts all your feeds in one place
readme_summary: Glance is a self-hosted, highly customizable dashboard designed to aggregate various feeds and information sources into a single, streamlined interface. It offers a fast, lightweight experience with low memory usage and a single binary for easy deployment, making it ideal for personal dashboards and monitoring.
key_features: Various widgets (RSS, Reddit, Hacker News, Weather, YouTube, Twitch, Market Prices, Docker status, Server stats, Custom)
Fast and lightweight with low memory usage and minimal dependencies
Highly customizable layouts, pages, and widget configurations
Themeable with custom CSS and pre-available themes
Single binary and small Docker container for easy deployment
primary_use_case: To provide users with a personalized, self-hosted dashboard that consolidates information from multiple sources like RSS feeds, social media, and system monitoring tools into a single, accessible interface.
cover_image_prompt: A digital librarian meticulously organizing a vast, glowing library where each book represents a different information feed (RSS, Reddit, YouTube, Docker stats). The librarian is placing these books onto a beautifully designed, minimalist shelf system that forms a cohesive dashboard interface. Subtle streams of data flow from the books to the shelf, indicating aggregation. Small, holographic screens around the librarian display snippets of the content from each feed. The overall mood is organized, efficient, and modern, with a clean, bright color palette. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clarity and a touch of futuristic elegance, subtly incorporating the 'Glance' name into a sign on the library wall.

name: Crucix
description: Your personal intelligence agent. Watches the world from multiple data sources and pings you when something changes.
readme_summary: Crucix is a personal intelligence agent that aggregates data from 27 open-source intelligence feeds, including satellite imagery, flight tracking, and economic indicators. It presents this information on a self-contained dashboard and can be integrated with LLMs to provide real-time alerts and respond to commands, acting as a personal analyst without cloud reliance.
key_features: Multi-tier alerts to Telegram and Discord
primary_use_case: To provide individuals with a comprehensive, real-time, and locally-run intelligence dashboard and assistant for monitoring global events and data.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant, futuristic analyst sitting at a sleek, minimalist desk, looking at a holographic globe displaying real-time data streams. The globe shows satellite fire detections, flight paths, and economic charts emanating from various global points. Small, glowing data particles flow from the globe towards the analyst, who is holding a tablet displaying a simple command interface like '/brief'. The background is a dimly lit, modern ops room with subtle code snippets and data visualizations on screens. The overall mood is one of focused awareness and control. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, high-tech aesthetic and a color palette of deep blues, cyans, and subtle oranges.

name: deepagents
readme_summary: Deep Agents is a Python agent harness built on LangGraph, designed for complex, long-horizon tasks. It provides an opinionated framework with features like sub-agents, filesystem access, and persistent memory, making it production-ready for various LLM applications.
key_features: Production-ready with streaming, persistence, checkpointing, tracing, and evaluation via LangSmith
primary_use_case: Developing and deploying sophisticated AI agents capable of multi-step, long-horizon tasks, with built-in support for complex operations like file manipulation and delegation.
cover_image_prompt: A master craftsman, akin to a clockmaker, meticulously assembling intricate gears and mechanisms that represent complex tasks. One gear is a 'sub-agent' that delegates work to smaller, specialized robots. Another gear is a 'filesystem' interface, showing digital blueprints being read and written. A glowing 'memory core' stores past work. The craftsman uses a 'human-in-the-loop' lever to approve critical steps. The scene is a well-lit, organized workshop with subtle digital particles flowing between components, hinting at AI. The repository name 'Deep Agents' is subtly etched on a workbench. Style: 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic and a color palette of blues, grays, and subtle gold accents.

name: project-nomad
description: Project N.O.M.A.D, is a self-contained, offline survival computer packed with critical tools, knowledge, and AI to keep you informed and empowered—anytime, anywhere.
readme_summary: Project N.O.M.A.D. is a self-contained, offline-first knowledge and education server that provides access to a curated collection of tools and resources. It leverages Docker to manage containerized applications, offering capabilities like AI chat with local knowledge bases, offline educational content, mapping, data tools, and system benchmarking, all accessible through a web-based Command Center.
key_features: AI Chat with local knowledge base (Ollama, RAG via Qdrant)
Information Library (Kiwix for Wikipedia, medical references, ebooks)
primary_use_case: To provide a self-contained, offline-accessible server for knowledge, education, and essential tools, empowering users with information and capabilities regardless of internet connectivity.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian in a cozy, dimly lit study, surrounded by towering shelves filled with physical books and glowing digital screens. The librarian is holding an open, ancient-looking tome, from which streams of light and data particles are flowing, transforming into vibrant, holographic educational modules, maps, and AI chat interfaces that hover in the air. Small, subtle icons representing Docker, Ollama, and Kiwix are etched into the wooden shelves. The scene evokes a sense of accessible, timeless knowledge, with a warm, inviting atmosphere. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration with rich textures and a focus on the magical transformation of information.
